version.description: Free-form schema/content version tag for this dataset.

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "heavy-hulls").

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the tech tree.

description.description: Flavor/UI text shown for this tech.

items.description: A named tech-tree node. The original encoded these as bit positions in a 400-bit mask with a parallel name table; here a tech is simply an id plus display metadata, and membership is expressed by TechSet.

mass.description: Ship mass; affects acceleration/inertia in the flight model.

signature.description: Base radar cross-section from the original Core model; the fog-of-war vision system instead uses radar-signature below.

speed.description: Maximum forward flight speed, in world units/second.

yaw.description: Rotation about the vertical axis (nose left/right), in degrees/second.

pitch.description: Rotation about the lateral axis (nose up/down), in degrees/second.

roll.description: Rotation about the longitudinal axis (barrel roll), in degrees/second.

max-turn-rates.description: Peak yaw/pitch/roll turn rates this hull can reach, in degrees/second.

yaw.description: Rotation about the vertical axis (nose left/right), in degrees/second.

pitch.description: Rotation about the lateral axis (nose up/down), in degrees/second.

roll.description: Rotation about the longitudinal axis (barrel roll), in degrees/second.

turn-torques.description: Legacy Core turn-acceleration stat (not currently used by the flight model); drift-*-deg below governs runtime turn feel instead.

strafe-thrust-multiplier.description: Fraction of forward thrust available when strafing sideways (1.0 = full thrust).

reverse-thrust-multiplier.description: Fraction of forward thrust available when reversing (1.0 = full thrust).

scanner-range.description: Legacy Core sensor-range stat; fog-of-war instead uses the vision-cone/vision-sphere fields below.

max-fuel.description: Afterburner fuel tank size; pairs with ab-fuel-drain/ab-fuel-recharge below (0 = no afterburner fuel modeled).

ecm.description: Legacy Core electronic-countermeasures stat (not currently used by the runtime).

length.description: Legacy Core hull length stat; model-length below sizes the runtime GLB instead.

max-energy.description: Legacy Core energy-pool stat (not currently used by the runtime).

energy-recharge-rate.description: Legacy Core energy-regen stat (not currently used by the runtime).

ripcord-speed.description: Legacy Core ripcord (emergency warp) departure speed (not currently used by the runtime).

ripcord-cost.description: Legacy Core ripcord (emergency warp) money cost (not currently used by the runtime).

max-ammo.description: Legacy Core max carried ammo stat (not currently used by the runtime).

armor-hit-points.description: Hull points before the ship is destroyed.

max-weapons.description: Legacy Core cap on mountable weapons (not currently enforced by the runtime, which derives loadout from hardpoints).

max-fixed-weapons.description: Legacy Core cap on fixed (forward-firing) weapons (not currently enforced by the runtime).

defense-type.description: Defense table id used to resolve incoming damage against armor.

magazine-capacity.description: Legacy Core magazine (missile rack) capacity stat (not currently used by the runtime).

dispenser-capacity.description: Legacy Core dispenser (mine/chaff pack) capacity stat (not currently used by the runtime).

chaff-launcher-capacity.description: Legacy Core chaff-launcher capacity stat (not currently used by the runtime).

successor-hull-id.description: Hull upgrade target; references another hull id.

preferred-parts.description: Suggested default loadout — references part ids.

allowed-parts.description: Per-slot whitelist of mountable parts: for each EquipmentSlot, the part ids that may be mounted there. Replaces the C++ pmEquipment[ET_MAX] part-mask array.

items.description: Capability flags a hull can carry. Modeled as a collection rather than a bitfield. Mirrors HullAbilityBitMask (igc.h:725-741).

abilities.description: Capability/role flags this hull carries (e.g. is-fighter, is-lifepod, no-ripcord).

class-id.description: Stable wire class id for this hull as a playable ship (Scout 0 / Fighter 1 / Bomber 2 / Pod 255). Null = not a runtime-playable hull. The game's ShipClass enum + content id constants depend on these exact bytes, so they are authored explicitly (not derived).

glyph.description: Hangar presentation flavor (projected onto the ShipClassDef; Description supplies the blurb). Glyph is the UI icon glyph, Role the short role tag (e.g. RECON). Both omit-when-null; empty falls back to a generic client default.

role.description: Short hangar role tag (e.g. RECON, INTERCEPT, ASSAULT); empty falls back to a generic client default.

model-length.description: Longest local axis (world units) the client uniform-scales the loaded hull GLB to (its silhouette length). Also sizes the engine glow and the loadout preview camera. Projected onto ShipClassDef.ModelLength.

payload-capacity.description: Total payload budget the hull can carry: the summed Mass of mounted weapons plus the cargo hold (expendable Mass × count). 0 = no hold (e.g. the pod). Runtime hulls with weapon hardpoints must author enough capacity for their default loadout — CoreValidator enforces this at load.

ore-capacity.description: Ore hold size (helium-3 units) for a mining hull: the harvest capacity a miner fills at a rock and offloads at a friendly base. 0 = not a miner (no ore hold). Independent of PayloadCapacity (weapons/cargo budget) — an ore hull is typically unarmed. Omit-when-default; projected onto ShipClassDef.OreCapacity.

drift-yaw-deg.description: Drift (turn-rate slop) knobs the game's flight model needs; no clean Core source.

drift-pitch-deg.description: Pitch-axis drift (turn-rate slop) knob, in degrees; pairs with drift-yaw-deg above.

ab-accel.description: Afterburner flight knobs (extra accel + spool on/off rates); no clean Core source.

ab-on-rate.description: How fast the afterburner boost spools up to full extra accel once engaged (per second).

ab-off-rate.description: How fast the afterburner boost spools back down once released (per second).

ab-fuel-drain.description: Afterburner fuel drain/recharge (per second); pairs with the Core MaxFuel field above.

ab-fuel-recharge.description: Afterburner fuel regained per second while boost is released (0 = dock-only refill, no in-flight regen).

shield-capacity.description: Regenerating energy shield layered over the raw hull. ShieldCapacity is the total shield pool (0 = this hull has NO shield). Incoming damage depletes the shield before the hull and overflows into the hull when the shield pops. ShieldRecharge is the regen rate in points/second, resuming ShieldDelay seconds after the last shield damage. All omit-when-default; projected onto the ShipClassDef shield fields.

shield-recharge.description: Shield regen rate, in points/second, once recharge resumes.

shield-delay.description: Seconds after the last shield hit before recharge resumes.

vision-cone-length.description: Long-range directional sensor cone: VisionConeLength is its max range (u), VisionConeAngleDeg its half-angle (degrees); asteroids occlude it. Paired with an omnidirectional VisionSphereRadius proximity sensor (unoccluded, shorter range). RadarSignature is a detection-range multiplier applied to every viewer's range against THIS hull (0/omitted -> 1.0 at projection; <1 stealthier, >1 easier to spot). All omit-when-default; projected onto the ShipClassDef vision fields.

vision-cone-angle-deg.description: Half-angle of the forward vision cone, in degrees.

vision-sphere-radius.description: Radius of the unoccluded omnidirectional proximity sensor, in world units.

radar-signature.description: Detection-range multiplier applied to every viewer's range against this hull (omitted/0 resolves to 1.0; below 1 is stealthier, above 1 easier to spot).

kind.description: What this hardpoint attaches (weapon muzzle, engine nozzle, light, docking marker, etc.).

index.description: Disambiguates multiples of one kind (e.g. two boosters).

off-x.description: Local-space X offset from the hull origin. Null = inherit the mesh node's position.

off-y.description: Local-space Y offset from the hull origin. Null = inherit the mesh node's position.

off-z.description: Local-space Z offset from the hull origin. Null = inherit the mesh node's position.

dir-x.description: Local-space X component of the hardpoint's facing direction. Null = inherit the mesh node's forward.

dir-y.description: Local-space Y component of the hardpoint's facing direction. Null = inherit the mesh node's forward.

dir-z.description: Local-space Z component of the hardpoint's facing direction. Null = inherit the mesh node's forward.

weapon-id.description: Meaningful only for Weapon; references a runtime weapon id.

items.description: A mount point on a hull/station, in LOCAL space — mirrors the game's HardpointDef. Carries what a loader needs to attach a weapon muzzle, engine nozzle, light or docking marker without any hard-coded offset. Declaration order of RuntimeHardpointKind fixes the wire byte, so it is APPEND-ONLY.

hardpoints.description: Local-space mount points (weapon muzzles, engine nozzles, lights) the client renders from.

item.description: References an Id that carries a cargo-id.

count.description: How many units of that expendable the hull spawns with.

items.description: One entry in a hull's default consumable hold: an expendable id + a count.

default-cargo.description: Default consumable hold this hull spawns with: each entry names an expendable (by id) and a count. Consumes payload-capacity alongside mounted weapon mass — CoreValidator proves the summed loadout fits at load. Omit-when-empty. Projected onto ShipClassDef.DefaultCargo (resolving each expendable id to its cargo-id).

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "heavy-fighter").

description.description: Flavor/blurb text shown for this item in the build UI.

model-name.description: Art asset id for the 3D model (see GLB-AND-HARDPOINT-FORMAT.md).

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the build-UI/HUD icon.

price.description: Cost in team money to build/buy this item.

group.description: Optional grouping id used to bucket related items in the build UI.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

required-capabilities.description: Capability gates that must all be enabled before this can be built/bought.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

granted-capabilities.description: Capability gates enabled for the team once this is owned/completed.

required-techs.description: Research techs that must all be owned before this can be built/bought.

granted-techs.description: Research techs granted to the team once this is owned/completed.

items.description: A ship chassis. Mirrors the C++ DataHullTypeIGC (igc.h:1767). Sound ids and the trailing hardpoint data from the original struct are intentionally omitted here — model geometry / hardpoints are covered separately (GLB-AND-HARDPOINT-FORMAT.md).

burst-time.description: Duration of a burst, in seconds (dtimeBurst).

energy-per-shot.description: Energy consumed by the ship each time this weapon fires.

dispersion.description: Random aiming spread applied to each shot, in radians.

ammo-per-shot.description: Rounds of ammo consumed per shot.

projectile-id.description: The projectile this weapon fires; references a projectile id.

weapon-id.description: Stable wire weapon id a hull hardpoint references (Scout 0 / Fighter 1 / Bomber 2). Null = not a runtime-fired weapon. Authored explicitly (the game's content id constants depend on it).

kind.description: Server-side firing behaviour dispatch; today every weapon is a Bolt.

fire-interval-ticks.description: Tick-domain ballistics, authored directly to avoid seconds→tick rounding drift.

projectile-life-ticks.description: How many ticks a fired projectile survives before expiring.

can-damage-base.description: True if this weapon's shots/warheads apply damage to bases (station siege ordnance).

mass.description: Mass this part adds when mounted, counted against the hull's payload capacity.

signature.description: Detectability this part adds to the mounting hull/station.

slot.description: Which hull slot this part occupies.

successor-part-id.description: Part upgrade target; references another part id.

shield-damage-multiplier.description: For a runtime WEAPON (a Weapon gun or a Launcher missile/mine): damage this weapon deals to an energy SHIELD relative to hull. 1.0 = equal; >1 strong vs shields, <1 weak. Null = default 1.0 (omit-when-default). Projected onto WeaponDef.ShieldMult; ignored for non-weapon parts.

model-name.description: Art asset id for the 3D model (see GLB-AND-HARDPOINT-FORMAT.md).

items.description: A gun. Mirrors the C++ DataWeaponTypeIGC (igc.h:1833).

regen-rate.description: How much shield strength regenerates per second.

max-strength.description: Maximum shield strength this generator can hold.

defense-type.description: Defense table id used to resolve incoming damage against the shield.

mass.description: Mass this part adds when mounted, counted against the hull's payload capacity.

signature.description: Detectability this part adds to the mounting hull/station.

slot.description: Which hull slot this part occupies.

successor-part-id.description: Part upgrade target; references another part id.

max-cloaking.description: Maximum cloaking strength (0..1 fraction of signature hidden).

on-rate.description: Rate the cloak engages, per second.

off-rate.description: Rate the cloak disengages, per second.

fuel-consumption.description: Fuel drained per second while the afterburner is engaged.

max-thrust.description: Peak extra thrust multiplier the afterburner provides at full engagement.

on-rate.description: Rate the afterburner engages toward max thrust, per second.

off-rate.description: Rate the afterburner disengages back to normal thrust, per second.

mass.description: Mass this part adds when mounted, counted against the hull's payload capacity.

signature.description: Detectability this part adds to the mounting hull/station.

slot.description: Which hull slot this part occupies.

items.description: A consumable booster pack. Mirrors the C++ DataPackTypeIGC (igc.h:1875).

amount.description: How many rounds the launcher holds.

launch-count.description: How many are released per launch.

expendable-id.description: The expendable this launcher fires; references an expendable id.

weapon-id.description: Stable wire weapon id a hull hardpoint references. Null = not a runtime-fired launcher. A launcher with a weapon id projects to a Missile WeaponDef whose ballistics come from the referenced ExpendableId missile. Authored explicitly (the game's content id constants depend on it), sharing the weapon-id namespace.

fire-interval-ticks.description: Tick-domain launch cadence, authored directly to avoid seconds→tick rounding drift.

mass.description: Mass this part adds when mounted, counted against the hull's payload capacity.

signature.description: Detectability this part adds to the mounting hull/station.

slot.description: Which hull slot this part occupies.

successor-part-id.description: Part upgrade target; references another part id.

required-techs.description: Research techs that must all be owned before this can be built/bought.

granted-techs.description: Research techs granted to the team once this is owned/completed.

items.description: A launcher that carries and fires expendables (a magazine, dispenser, or chaff launcher). Mirrors the C++ DataLauncherTypeIGC (igc.h:1881); its Slot selects which kind it is (Magazine / Dispenser / ChaffLauncher).

signature.description: Base radar cross-section (detectability) stat; the fog-of-war vision system instead uses radar-signature below.

max-armor.description: Hull points before the station's armor is depleted.

max-shield.description: Size of the station's regenerating shield pool (0 = no shield).

scanner-range.description: Legacy Core sensor-range stat; fog-of-war instead uses vision-sphere-radius below.

radius.description: Physical collision/docking radius of the station, in world units.

class.description: Functional category of this station (garrison, shipyard, mining, etc).

local-techs.description: Techs granted only locally (while/where this station exists), distinct from the global GrantedTechs. Mirrors the C++ ttbmLocal.

successor-station-id.description: Station upgrade target; references another station id.

defense-type-armor.description: Defense table id used to resolve incoming damage against armor.

defense-type-shield.description: Defense table id used to resolve incoming damage against shield.

items.description: Capability flags a station can carry. Mirrors StationAbilityBitMask (igc.h:743-758).

abilities.description: Capability flags this station carries (e.g. start, restart, repair, capture, land).

items.description: What can be done with / built on an asteroid. Mirrors the C++ c_aabm* flags (AGCIDL.idl:389-393).

buildable-on.description: Which asteroid kinds this station may be built on. Mirrors aabmBuild.

construction-drone-id.description: The construction drone this station produces; references a drone id.

base-type-id.description: Stable wire base-type id for this station as a runtime base (Garrison 0). Null = not a runtime base. Authored explicitly (the game's content id constants depend on it).

items.description: A mount point on a hull/station, in LOCAL space — mirrors the game's HardpointDef. Carries what a loader needs to attach a weapon muzzle, engine nozzle, light or docking marker without any hard-coded offset. Declaration order of RuntimeHardpointKind fixes the wire byte, so it is APPEND-ONLY.

hardpoints.description: Local-space mount points (docking entrance/exit, nav lights) the client renders from.

vision-sphere-radius.description: Omnidirectional, unoccluded vision sphere this base contributes to its owning team (the garrison watches its surroundings from tick 0). RadarSignature is a detection-range multiplier applied to every viewer's range against this base (0/omitted -> 1.0 at projection). Both omit-when-default; projected onto BaseDef.

radar-signature.description: Detection-range multiplier applied to every viewer's range against this base (omitted/0 resolves to 1.0).

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "heavy-fighter").

description.description: Flavor/blurb text shown for this item in the build UI.

model-name.description: Art asset id for the 3D model (see GLB-AND-HARDPOINT-FORMAT.md).

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the build-UI/HUD icon.

price.description: Cost in team money to build/buy this item.

group.description: Optional grouping id used to bucket related items in the build UI.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

required-capabilities.description: Capability gates that must all be enabled before this can be built/bought.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

granted-capabilities.description: Capability gates enabled for the team once this is owned/completed.

required-techs.description: Research techs that must all be owned before this can be built/bought.

granted-techs.description: Research techs granted to the team once this is owned/completed.

items.description: A building / station. Mirrors the C++ DataStationTypeIGC (igc.h:2649).

attributes.description: Multiplicative stat modifiers granted to the team while this is owned.

tech-only.description: When true this development exists only to grant techs (no lasting effect of its own), so it becomes obsolete once its GrantedTechs are already owned. Mirrors the C++ tech-only / IsObsolete behaviour (developmentigc.h:110).

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "heavy-fighter").

description.description: Flavor/blurb text shown for this item in the build UI.

model-name.description: Art asset id for the 3D model (see GLB-AND-HARDPOINT-FORMAT.md).

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the build-UI/HUD icon.

price.description: Cost in team money to build/buy this item.

group.description: Optional grouping id used to bucket related items in the build UI.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

required-capabilities.description: Capability gates that must all be enabled before this can be built/bought.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

granted-capabilities.description: Capability gates enabled for the team once this is owned/completed.

required-techs.description: Research techs that must all be owned before this can be built/bought.

granted-techs.description: Research techs granted to the team once this is owned/completed.

items.description: A research / tech purchase. Mirrors the C++ DataDevelopmentIGC (igc.h:2691). Besides flipping GrantedTechs, it can apply team-wide stat modifiers.

bravery.description: AI willingness to engage/hold in combat, from 0 (timid) to 1 (fearless).

pilot.description: Behaviour profile this drone's AI follows (miner, wingman, layer, builder).

hull-id.description: The hull this drone flies; references a hull id.

deployed-expendable-id.description: What this drone lays/deploys (e.g. mines); references an expendable id.

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "heavy-fighter").

description.description: Flavor/blurb text shown for this item in the build UI.

model-name.description: Art asset id for the 3D model (see GLB-AND-HARDPOINT-FORMAT.md).

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the build-UI/HUD icon.

price.description: Cost in team money to build/buy this item.

group.description: Optional grouping id used to bucket related items in the build UI.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

required-capabilities.description: Capability gates that must all be enabled before this can be built/bought.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

granted-capabilities.description: Capability gates enabled for the team once this is owned/completed.

required-techs.description: Research techs that must all be owned before this can be built/bought.

granted-techs.description: Research techs granted to the team once this is owned/completed.

items.description: An AI-piloted ship (miner, constructor, turret, bot). Mirrors DataDroneTypeIGC (igc.h:2698).

acceleration.description: Thrust acceleration once fired, in u/s².

initial-speed.description: Speed the missile launches at, in u/s.

lock-time.description: Seconds the target must stay in-cone before the missile achieves lock.

ready-time.description: Cooldown after firing before the launcher can lock another missile, in seconds.

chaff-resistance.description: How strongly the missile resists being decoyed by chaff (compared against chaff strength).

dispersion.description: Aim/trajectory randomization applied to the missile.

power.description: Damage dealt by a direct (fuse-triggering) hit, before the direct-hit multiplier.

blast-power.description: Splash damage dealt to other ships within the blast radius of the detonation.

blast-radius.description: Splash-damage cutoff radius around the detonation point, in u; serializes as blast-radius.

damage-type.description: Damage-type category this missile's warhead deals.

directional.description: True if the warhead is directional (shaped) rather than radial.

max-speed.description: Speed cap once boosted, in u/s; 0 = uncapped (projected onto WeaponDef.MissileMaxSpeed).

direct-hit-multiplier.description: Damage multiplier applied to Power on a direct (fuse-triggering) hit.

model-name.description: GLB model basename the client loads from assets/missiles/ (no extension).

trail-lifetime.description: Smoke-trail plume lifetime, in seconds (client EngineGlow tuning).

trail-scale.description: Smoke-trail plume size scale (client EngineGlow tuning).

trail-color.description: Smoke-trail tint as a 6- or 8-digit hex string (RRGGBB[AA]); parsed to u32 at projection.

can-damage-base.description: True if this missile's warhead applies damage to bases (station siege ordnance).

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "seeker-missile").

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the HUD/cargo-list icon.

description.description: Flavor/UI text shown for the expendable.

mass.description: Payload units one carried unit occupies in a hull's hold (see PayloadCapacity).

load-time.description: Time to load/ready the expendable, in seconds.

lifespan.description: How long it lives once released, in seconds.

signature.description: Radar signature the expendable presents to enemy detection while active.

hit-points.description: Hit points before the expendable is destroyed; 0 = invulnerable.

defense-type.description: Damage-type category this expendable counts as when it deals or absorbs damage.

items.description: Capability flags for consumables (missiles, mines, etc.). Mirrors ExpendableAbilityBitMask (igc.h:770-782).

abilities.description: Special capability flags granted to this expendable.

cargo-id.description: Stable wire id for this expendable as a runtime cargo item the hangar can stock. Null = not a runtime cargo item. Authored explicitly, like ClassId / weapon ids.

glyph.description: Single-character UI glyph the hangar cargo list renders.

charges-per-pack.description: Charges dispensed from one loaded pack; the hangar loads a number of PACKS (each costing one Mass) and every press distributes one charge. Total charges = packs × charges-per-pack. Null → 1 (a pack is a single charge — legacy one-for-one behavior).

items.description: A guided missile. Mirrors the C++ DataMissileTypeIGC (igc.h:1960).

radius.description: Trigger/blast radius of a single deployed mine, in u.

power.description: Damage per second dealt to an enemy inside the mine field, scaled by the victim's speed.

endurance.description: How long an armed mine field persists before expiring, in seconds.

cloud-radius.description: Radius of the pseudo-random cloud the field scatters its mines within, in u (projected onto WeaponDef.MineCloudRadius).

cloud-count.description: How many mines a single deploy scatters into the field (projected onto WeaponDef.MineCloudCount; capped at 64 by the seed-based aliveMask wire).

arm-delay.description: Field arm delay, in seconds — mines are inert until this elapses (projected onto WeaponDef.MineArmTicks after ×20 tick conversion).

model-name.description: GLB model basename the client instances (once per visual mine) from assets/mines/ (no extension); projected onto WeaponDef.ModelName.

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "seeker-missile").

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the HUD/cargo-list icon.

description.description: Flavor/UI text shown for the expendable.

mass.description: Payload units one carried unit occupies in a hull's hold (see PayloadCapacity).

load-time.description: Time to load/ready the expendable, in seconds.

lifespan.description: How long it lives once released, in seconds.

signature.description: Radar signature the expendable presents to enemy detection while active.

hit-points.description: Hit points before the expendable is destroyed; 0 = invulnerable.

defense-type.description: Damage-type category this expendable counts as when it deals or absorbs damage.

items.description: Capability flags for consumables (missiles, mines, etc.). Mirrors ExpendableAbilityBitMask (igc.h:770-782).

abilities.description: Special capability flags granted to this expendable.

cargo-id.description: Stable wire id for this expendable as a runtime cargo item the hangar can stock. Null = not a runtime cargo item. Authored explicitly, like ClassId / weapon ids.

glyph.description: Single-character UI glyph the hangar cargo list renders.

charges-per-pack.description: Charges dispensed from one loaded pack; the hangar loads a number of PACKS (each costing one Mass) and every press distributes one charge. Total charges = packs × charges-per-pack. Null → 1 (a pack is a single charge — legacy one-for-one behavior).

items.description: A deployable mine. Mirrors the C++ DataMineTypeIGC (igc.h:1990).

chaff-strength.description: How strongly it decoys missile locks.

decoy-radius.description: Radius, in u, within which a missile can be decoyed onto this puff (projected onto WeaponDef.DecoyRadius).

model-name.description: GLB model basename the client loads from assets/chaff/ (no extension); projected onto WeaponDef.ModelName.

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "seeker-missile").

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the HUD/cargo-list icon.

description.description: Flavor/UI text shown for the expendable.

mass.description: Payload units one carried unit occupies in a hull's hold (see PayloadCapacity).

load-time.description: Time to load/ready the expendable, in seconds.

lifespan.description: How long it lives once released, in seconds.

signature.description: Radar signature the expendable presents to enemy detection while active.

hit-points.description: Hit points before the expendable is destroyed; 0 = invulnerable.

defense-type.description: Damage-type category this expendable counts as when it deals or absorbs damage.

items.description: Capability flags for consumables (missiles, mines, etc.). Mirrors ExpendableAbilityBitMask (igc.h:770-782).

abilities.description: Special capability flags granted to this expendable.

cargo-id.description: Stable wire id for this expendable as a runtime cargo item the hangar can stock. Null = not a runtime cargo item. Authored explicitly, like ClassId / weapon ids.

glyph.description: Single-character UI glyph the hangar cargo list renders.

charges-per-pack.description: Charges dispensed from one loaded pack; the hangar loads a number of PACKS (each costing one Mass) and every press distributes one charge. Total charges = packs × charges-per-pack. Null → 1 (a pack is a single charge — legacy one-for-one behavior).

items.description: Countermeasure chaff. Mirrors the C++ DataChaffTypeIGC (igc.h:1998).

scanner-range.description: Detection range of the probe's onboard scanner, in u.

burst-time.description: Duration of an armed probe's firing burst, in seconds.

dispersion.description: Aim randomization applied to the probe's fired shots.

accuracy.description: Hit-chance/aim precision of the probe's turret fire.

ammo.description: Number of shots the armed probe's turret carries.

projectile-id.description: Projectile fired by an armed probe; references a projectile id.

sight-radius.description: Radius of the team vision sphere this deployed probe grants while alive, in u (projected onto WeaponDef.ProbeSightRadius).

model-name.description: GLB model basename the client instances (once per deployed probe) from assets/probes/ (no extension); projected onto WeaponDef.ModelName.

hit-radius.description: Server hit-sphere radius for bolts/blasts against the deployed probe, in u (projected onto WeaponDef.ProbeHitRadius). Required when hit-points > 0 makes the probe destructible. HitPoints and Signature live on the Expendable base.

model-size.description: Client visual normalization length for the probe model, in u (projected onto WeaponDef.ProbeModelSize). 0/omitted keeps the client's guard default.

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "seeker-missile").

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the HUD/cargo-list icon.

description.description: Flavor/UI text shown for the expendable.

mass.description: Payload units one carried unit occupies in a hull's hold (see PayloadCapacity).

load-time.description: Time to load/ready the expendable, in seconds.

lifespan.description: How long it lives once released, in seconds.

signature.description: Radar signature the expendable presents to enemy detection while active.

hit-points.description: Hit points before the expendable is destroyed; 0 = invulnerable.

defense-type.description: Damage-type category this expendable counts as when it deals or absorbs damage.

items.description: Capability flags for consumables (missiles, mines, etc.). Mirrors ExpendableAbilityBitMask (igc.h:770-782).

abilities.description: Special capability flags granted to this expendable.

cargo-id.description: Stable wire id for this expendable as a runtime cargo item the hangar can stock. Null = not a runtime cargo item. Authored explicitly, like ClassId / weapon ids.

glyph.description: Single-character UI glyph the hangar cargo list renders.

charges-per-pack.description: Charges dispensed from one loaded pack; the hangar loads a number of PACKS (each costing one Mass) and every press distributes one charge. Total charges = packs × charges-per-pack. Null → 1 (a pack is a single charge — legacy one-for-one behavior).

items.description: A deployable sensor / turret probe. Mirrors the C++ DataProbeTypeIGC (igc.h:2003).

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "scout-bolt").

power.description: Damage dealt on impact; projects onto the owning weapon's damage.

speed.description: Bolt travel speed, in world units/second.

lifespan.description: Legacy Core seconds-before-expiry stat; the owning weapon's tick-domain projectile-life-ticks governs runtime lifespan instead.

width.description: Collision radius of the bolt, in world units; projects onto the weapon's projectile radius.

damage-type.description: Damage-type category this projectile counts as when it deals damage (not currently used by the runtime).

bolt-radius.description: Client bolt-mesh visual dimensions (world units); folded into WeaponDef and streamed. 0 = the client's built-in default bolt size.

bolt-length.description: Client bolt-mesh visual length (world units); 0 = the client's built-in default bolt size.

items.description: A gun/probe projectile definition. Mirrors the C++ DataProjectileTypeIGC (igc.h:1924). Referenced by ProjectileId and ProjectileId.

id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "ucn").

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the faction's icon/crest shown in faction-select UI.

hud-name.description: HUD theme/skin id for this faction.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

base-capabilities.description: Capability gates the faction starts the match with — the seed of its tech tree.

base-techs.description: Research techs the faction starts the match with.

no-dev-techs.description: Techs available even without buying developments.

base-attributes.description: Baseline team-wide stat multipliers before any research.

lifepod-hull-id.description: The hull a pilot ejects into; references a hull id.

initial-station-id.description: The faction's starting base; references a station id.

items.description: A playable faction template. Mirrors the C++ DataCivilizationIGC (igc.h:2071). A faction does not own ships/parts directly — it connects to the shared catalog indirectly through its starting techs (BaseTechs), which unlock a subset of the catalog.

description: A complete static data set: the shared catalog of buyables/expendables plus the factions that draw on it. Equivalent to the contents of a C++ "static core" (.igc) file, but represented as clean object lists. Loaded from / saved to YAML (split across files via a manifest).
